tool_args.label: Malcolm X Blvd / Utica Ave Transit Improvements – CB3 Transportation Committee May 2019

payload.title: Malcolm X Blvd / Utica Ave Transit Improvements
payload.description: Presentation to Brooklyn Community Board 3 Transportation Committee on May 14, 2019, covering project background, existing conditions, proposal, and next steps for transit improvements on Malcolm X Blvd and Utica Ave.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: New York City Department of Transportation, publisher and presenter of the Malcolm X Blvd / Utica Ave Transit Improvements presentation.
Community Board 3 Transportation Committee, audience for the May 14, 2019 presentation.

tool_args.label: Malcolm X Blvd / Utica Ave Transit Improvements

payload.project_name: Malcolm X Blvd / Utica Ave Transit Improvements
payload.description: 2019 priority project identified in the Better Buses Action Plan. Includes curbside bus lane (Fulton St to Atlantic Ave, southbound), queue jump signal at Utica Ave & Atlantic Ave, B46 upgrade to articulated buses, and future curbside bus lane (Chauncey St to Fulton St, southbound).

warnings: project.program is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Program names caused identity overmerge; they are relation context, not strong project identity. Suggested relation: part_of_program.
project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

payload.description: B46 Select Bus Service, Brooklyn's busiest bus route with 38,000 daily riders. Launched July 2016. Connects Bed-Stuy to subway lines and 30 bus routes.
B46 local bus route in Brooklyn.
Corridor in Brooklyn identified as a 2019 priority project for transit improvements including potential bus lanes.

Segment from Chauncey St to Atlantic Ave where transit improvements including curbside bus lane are proposed for B46. Dept. of Education Authorized Parking Only 7am-4pm Mon-Fri, average parking occupancy 7% 7am-7pm.
3 blocks of existing bus lanes approaching Atlantic Ave installed in 2016 (northbound). Bus speeds improved 17-26% during peak. Queue jump signal proposed at Utica Ave & Atlantic Ave.

tool_args.label: Curbside Bus Lane, Fulton St to Atlantic Ave (southbound)

payload.location_text: Fulton St to Atlantic Ave, southbound only
payload.description: Proposed curbside bus lane on Fulton St from Utica Ave to Atlantic Ave (southbound only). Overnight parking remains. Allows buses to bypass traffic approaching Atlantic Ave.

normalized_location.raw_text: Fulton St to Atlantic Ave, southbound only

tool_args.label: Queue Jump Signal, Utica Ave & Atlantic Ave

payload.description: 7-second head start for buses and pedestrians crossing Atlantic Ave. Pedestrian safety improvement on a Vision Zero Priority Corridor.
B46 to upgrade to longer, articulated buses in 2019/2020. After upgrade, install curbside bus lane Chauncey St to Fulton St (southbound only).

tool_args.label: Curbside Bus Lane, Chauncey St to Fulton St (southbound)

payload.location_text: Chauncey St to Fulton St, southbound only
payload.description: Proposed curbside bus lane after B46 upgrades to articulated buses. Overnight parking remains. Allows buses to bypass traffic approaching Atlantic Ave.

normalized_location.raw_text: Chauncey St to Fulton St, southbound only

payload.description: 5-month outreach effort. Visited 27 venues in CB 3. 619 participants took a survey. Conversations with 800+ members of the public.
